results.title: Protein language model embeddings for fast, accurate, alignment-free protein structure prediction
results.tldr: This work has described the first competitive AI solution exclusively inputting embeddings extracted from pre-trained protein Language Models, namely from the transformer pLM ProtT5, from single sequences into a relatively shallow convolutional neural network trained on inter-residue distances.
results.abstract: This work has described the first competitive AI solution exclusively inputting embeddings extracted from pre-trained protein Language Models, namely from the transformer pLM ProtT5, from single sequences into a relatively shallow convolutional neural network trained on inter-residue distances.

results.title: A Transformer-Based Ensemble Framework for the Prediction of Protein–Protein Interaction Sites
results.tldr: A novel ensemble framework for PPI sites prediction, EnsemPPIS, was proposed based on transformer and gated convolutional networks that was unique in extracting residue interactions from protein sequences with transformer and further integrating global and local sequential features with the ensemble learning strategy.
results.abstract: A novel ensemble framework for PPI sites prediction, EnsemPPIS, was proposed based on transformer and gated convolutional networks that was unique in extracting residue interactions from protein sequences with transformer and further integrating global and local sequential features with the ensemble learning strategy.

results.title: DeepProSite: structure-aware protein binding site prediction using ESMFold and pretrained language model
results.tldr: In predicting protein–protein/peptide binding sites, DeepProSite outperforms state-of-the-art sequence- and structure-based methods on most metrics, and maintains its performance when predicting unbound structures, in contrast to competing structure-based prediction methods.
results.abstract: In predicting protein–protein/peptide binding sites, DeepProSite outperforms state-of-the-art sequence- and structure-based methods on most metrics, and maintains its performance when predicting unbound structures, in contrast to competing structure-based prediction methods.

results.title: Flexible protein–protein docking with a multitrack iterative transformer
results.tldr: GeoDock is a multitrack iterative transformer network to predict a docked structure from separate docking partners that outperforms the deep learning models trained using the same dataset but falls behind most of the conventional methods and AlphaFold‐Multimer.
results.abstract: GeoDock is a multitrack iterative transformer network to predict a docked structure from separate docking partners that outperforms the deep learning models trained using the same dataset but falls behind most of the conventional methods and AlphaFold‐Multimer.

results.venue: Journal of Chemical Information and Modeling
results.tldr: A novel computational approach, TransEFVP, based on large-scale protein language model embeddings and a transformer-based neural network to predict disease-associated SAVs, demonstrating that TransEFVP can be explored as an accurate and effective SAV pathogenicity prediction method.
results.abstract: A novel computational approach, TransEFVP, based on large-scale protein language model embeddings and a transformer-based neural network to predict disease-associated SAVs, demonstrating that TransEFVP can be explored as an accurate and effective SAV pathogenicity prediction method.

results.title: SPOT-Contact-LM: improving single-sequence-based prediction of protein contact map using a transformer language model
results.tldr: A contact-map predictor utilizing the output of a pre-trained language model ESM-1b as an input along with a large training set and an ensemble of residual neural networks is developed, providing a much faster and reasonably accurate alternative to evolution-based methods, useful for large-scale prediction.
results.abstract: A contact-map predictor utilizing the output of a pre-trained language model ESM-1b as an input along with a large training set and an ensemble of residual neural networks is developed, providing a much faster and reasonably accurate alternative to evolution-based methods, useful for large-scale prediction.

results.title: A Protein Structure Prediction Approach Leveraging Transformer and CNN Integration
results.venue: 2024 7th International Conference on Advanced Algorithms and Control Engineering (ICAACE)
results.tldr: A two-dimensional fusion deep neural network model, DstruCCN, is adopted, which uses Convolutional Neural Networks (CCN) and a supervised Transformer protein language model for single-sequence protein structure prediction.
results.abstract: A two-dimensional fusion deep neural network model, DstruCCN, is adopted, which uses Convolutional Neural Networks (CCN) and a supervised Transformer protein language model for single-sequence protein structure prediction.

results.title: GEFormerDTA: drug target affinity prediction based on transformer graph for early fusion
results.tldr: A Transformer graph-based early fusion research approach for drug-target affinity prediction (GEFormerDTA) that reduces prediction errors caused by insufficient feature learning and Experimental results showed a better prediction of drugtarget affinity than existing affinity prediction methods.
results.abstract: A Transformer graph-based early fusion research approach for drug-target affinity prediction (GEFormerDTA) that reduces prediction errors caused by insufficient feature learning and Experimental results showed a better prediction of drugtarget affinity than existing affinity prediction methods.

results.title: Atomic protein structure refinement using all-atom graph representations and SE(3)-equivariant graph transformer
results.tldr: ATOMRefine is a deep learning-based, end-to-end, all-atom protein structural model refinement method that uses a SE(3)-equivariant graph transformer network to directly refine protein atomic coordinates in a predicted tertiary structure represented as a molecular graph.
results.abstract: ATOMRefine is a deep learning-based, end-to-end, all-atom protein structural model refinement method that uses a SE(3)-equivariant graph transformer network to directly refine protein atomic coordinates in a predicted tertiary structure represented as a molecular graph.

results.title: Co-evolution Transformer for Protein Contact Prediction
results.tldr: An attention-based architecture, Co-evolution Transformer (CoT), is proposed, which jointly considers the information from all homologous sequences in the MSA to better capture global coevolutionary patterns.
results.abstract: An attention-based architecture, Co-evolution Transformer (CoT), is proposed, which jointly considers the information from all homologous sequences in the MSA to better capture global coevolutionary patterns.

results.title: Deep learning methods for protein structure prediction
results.tldr: The update of AlphaFold 3 further extends the boundaries of prediction models, especially in protein‐small molecule structure prediction, marking a key shift toward a holistic approach in biomolecular structure elucidation, aiming at solving almost all sequence‐to‐structure puzzles in various biological phenomena.
results.abstract: The update of AlphaFold 3 further extends the boundaries of prediction models, especially in protein‐small molecule structure prediction, marking a key shift toward a holistic approach in biomolecular structure elucidation, aiming at solving almost all sequence‐to‐structure puzzles in various biological phenomena.

results.tldr: The design of the GACT-PPIS algorithm aims to utilize combined information from protein sequences and structures as input to predict protein-protein interaction sites, and demonstrates excellent generalization and versatility across different test sets.

results.title: Single-sequence protein structure prediction using supervised transformer protein language models
results.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:p>
                  It remains challenging for single-sequence protein structure prediction with AlphaFold2 and other deep learning methods. In this work, we introduce trRosettaX-Single, a novel algorithm for singlesequence protein structure prediction. It is built on sequence embedding from s-ESM-1b, a supervised transformer protein language model optimized from the pre-trained model ESM-1b. The sequence embedding is fed into a multi-scal

results.title: Drug-Target Interaction Prediction By Combining Transformer and Graph Neural Networks
results.tldr: The proposed method validates the applicability of combining graph neural networks and transformer architecture in drug discovery, and due to the attention mechanisms, it can extract deep structure feature data of drugs and proteins.
results.abstract: The proposed method validates the applicability of combining graph neural networks and transformer architecture in drug discovery, and due to the attention mechanisms, it can extract deep structure feature data of drugs and proteins.

results.title: Transformer Encoder with Protein Language Model for Protein Secondary Structure Prediction
results.tldr: The findings reveal that TE_SS excels in nine- and three-class structure predictions while also showing remarkable proficiency in the eight-class category, providing a significant tool for protein structure analysis, thereby enriching the field of bioinformatics.
results.abstract: The findings reveal that TE_SS excels in nine- and three-class structure predictions while also showing remarkable proficiency in the eight-class category, providing a significant tool for protein structure analysis, thereby enriching the field of bioinformatics.

results.title: Tasser‐Based Protein Structure Prediction
Improving protein secondary structure prediction by deep language models and transformer networks
results.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:p>
                  Protein secondary structure prediction is useful for many applications. It can be considered a language translation problem, i.e., translating a sequence of 20 different amino acids into a sequence of secondary structure symbols (e.g., alpha helix, beta strand, and coil). Here, we develop a novel protein secondary structure predictor called TransPross based on the transformer network and attention mechanism widely used
results.tldr: The attention mechnism used by TransPross can effectively capture long-range residue-residue interactions in protein sequences to predict secondary structures and outperforms the state-of-art methods.

Dynamic integration of feature- and template-based methods improves the prediction of conformational B cell epitopes.
results.tldr: DynaBCE, a dynamic ensemble algorithm to effectively identify conformational B cell epitopes (BCEs), is presented, which achieved promising results for both native and predicted structures and outperformed previous methods as demonstrated in various evaluation scenarios.
results.abstract: DynaBCE, a dynamic ensemble algorithm to effectively identify conformational B cell epitopes (BCEs), is presented, which achieved promising results for both native and predicted structures and outperformed previous methods as demonstrated in various evaluation scenarios.

results.title: MCT-ARG: Identification and classification of antibiotic resistance genes based on a multi-channel Transformer model.
results.tldr: MCT-ARG, a multi-channel Transformer framework that integrates protein primary sequences, predicted secondary structure, and relative solvent accessibility to construct comprehensive multimodal representations for ARG prediction and mechanistic insight, exceeds current state-of-the-art models in predictive accuracy, robustness, and interpretability.
results.abstract: MCT-ARG, a multi-channel Transformer framework that integrates protein primary sequences, predicted secondary structure, and relative solvent accessibility to construct comprehensive multimodal representations for ARG prediction and mechanistic insight, exceeds current state-of-the-art models in predictive accuracy, robustness, and interpretability.

results.title: ProtT-Affinity: Sequence-Based Protein-Protein Binding Affinity Prediction Using ProtT5 Embeddings
results.tldr: Although its performance does not match the strongest structure based methods, it is competitive with several widely used approaches and provides a practical alternative when structural data are missing or uncertain and suggest that large protein language models capture features relevant to binding energetics, and that these features can be exploited to approximate affinity trends at scale.
results.abstract: Although its performance does not match the strongest structure based methods, it is competitive with several widely used approaches and provides a practical alternative when structural data are missing or uncertain and suggest that large protein language models capture features relevant to binding energetics, and that these features can be exploited to approximate affinity trends at scale.

results.title: CrysFormer: Protein Structure Prediction via 3d Patterson Maps and Partial Structure Attention
results.tldr: This work proposes the first transformer-based model that directly utilizes protein crystallography and partial structure information to predict the electron density maps of proteins, and demonstrates its method can achieve accurate predictions, based on a much smaller dataset size and with reduced computation costs.
results.abstract: This work proposes the first transformer-based model that directly utilizes protein crystallography and partial structure information to predict the electron density maps of proteins, and demonstrates its method can achieve accurate predictions, based on a much smaller dataset size and with reduced computation costs.

results.venue: Journal of Computational Methods in Sciences and Engineering
results.tldr: This work provides a modular, end-to-end solution for large-scale protein analysis and shows potential for extension to downstream tasks such as mutation effect prediction or protein–protein interaction modeling.
results.abstract: This work provides a modular, end-to-end solution for large-scale protein analysis and shows potential for extension to downstream tasks such as mutation effect prediction or protein–protein interaction modeling.

results.title: ChaiSite: An Accurate Method for Protein–Nucleic Acid Binding Site Prediction Based on a Complex Structure Prediction Network
results.venue: IEEE International Conference on Bioinformatics and Biomedicine

results.title: Protein Secondary Structure Prediction Using 3D Graphs and Relation-Aware Message Passing Transformers
results.tldr: This study adeptly combines Graph Neural Networks and Language Models, specifically utilizing a pre-trained transformer-based protein language model to encode amino acid sequences and employing message-passing mechanisms like GCN and R-GCN to capture geometric characteristics of protein structures.
results.abstract: This study adeptly combines Graph Neural Networks and Language Models, specifically utilizing a pre-trained transformer-based protein language model to encode amino acid sequences and employing message-passing mechanisms like GCN and R-GCN to capture geometric characteristics of protein structures.

results.title: An Energy-Adaptive Elastic Equivariant Transformer Framework for Protein Structure Representation
results.tldr: A novel equivariant Transformer-State Space Model(SSM) hybrid framework, termed E^3$former, designed for efficient protein representation, that uses energy function-based receptive fields to construct proximity graphs and incorporates an equivariant high-tensor-elastic selective SSM within the transformer architecture.
results.abstract: A novel equivariant Transformer-State Space Model(SSM) hybrid framework, termed E^3$former, designed for efficient protein representation, that uses energy function-based receptive fields to construct proximity graphs and incorporates an equivariant high-tensor-elastic selective SSM within the transformer architecture.

results.title: Protein Structure Prediction, Structural Bioinformatics and Deep Learning
results.venue: International Journal of Current Microbiology and Applied Sciences
results.tldr: A comprehensive detail of recent progress and challenges in deep learning-based protein structure prediction has been presented, and a benchmark system for structure prediction and visualization, enabling analysis of user-provided protein sequences has been introduced.
results.abstract: A comprehensive detail of recent progress and challenges in deep learning-based protein structure prediction has been presented, and a benchmark system for structure prediction and visualization, enabling analysis of user-provided protein sequences has been introduced.

results.title: SwinSite: 3D Structure-Based Prediction of Protein–Ligand Binding Sites Using a Combined Vision Transformer and Convolution Model
results.venue: Journal of Chemical Information and Modeling
results.tldr: Evaluations on multiple benchmark data sets demonstrate that SwinSite outperforms existing CNN- and GNN-based ligand binding site detection methods consistently, highlighting its robustness and generalization ability.
results.abstract: Evaluations on multiple benchmark data sets demonstrate that SwinSite outperforms existing CNN- and GNN-based ligand binding site detection methods consistently, highlighting its robustness and generalization ability.

results.title: DeepStruct: Multi-modal deep learning integrating omics and energy scoring for protein structure prediction in plants
results.tldr: Results show that contextual biological signal integration can complement sequence-driven structural prediction, especially for proteins that are conditioned by environmental stress.
results.abstract: Results show that contextual biological signal integration can complement sequence-driven structural prediction, especially for proteins that are conditioned by environmental stress.

results.title: Structure-aware graph attention based hierarchical transformer framework for drug-target binding affinity prediction
results.tldr: This approach comprehensively models both intramolecular structural features and intermolecular interactions, thereby enhancing binding affinity prediction performance, and surpasses the state-of-the-art methods under challenging target cold-start settings.
results.abstract: This approach comprehensively models both intramolecular structural features and intermolecular interactions, thereby enhancing binding affinity prediction performance, and surpasses the state-of-the-art methods under challenging target cold-start settings.

results.title: TransGNN-DTA: A Framework for Drug-Target Affinity Prediction Based on a Chunked Transformer-GNN
results.tldr: The TransGNN-DTA framework is proposed to construct a multi-scale feature representation and dynamic fusion mechanism by hierarchically integrating Transformer and graph neural network, providing a scalable end-to-end solution for large-scale DTA prediction.
results.abstract: The TransGNN-DTA framework is proposed to construct a multi-scale feature representation and dynamic fusion mechanism by hierarchically integrating Transformer and graph neural network, providing a scalable end-to-end solution for large-scale DTA prediction.

results.title: Agnostic Biomolecular Binding Affinity Prediction via Frame Averaging Graph Transformer
results.venue: IEEE International Conference on Bioinformatics and Biomedicine

results.title: Navigating the uncharted: AI-driven advances in protein structure, dynamics, interactions and ligand interactions for understudied families.
results.tldr: This review traces the evolution of conventional methods to cutting-edge deep learning frameworks, such as AlphaFold2, RoseTTAFold, and transformer-based architectures, and explores how these AI-driven tools achieve near-experimental accuracy in structure prediction, model protein dynamics, and intrinsic disorder.
results.abstract: This review traces the evolution of conventional methods to cutting-edge deep learning frameworks, such as AlphaFold2, RoseTTAFold, and transformer-based architectures, and explores how these AI-driven tools achieve near-experimental accuracy in structure prediction, model protein dynamics, and intrinsic disorder.

results.title: MotifGT-DTI: Pivotal Motif-Based Graph Transformer Model Improves Drug--Target Interaction Prediction
results.venue: IEEE Transactions on Neural Networks and Learning Systems

results.title: Explainable Deep Learning Framework for Drug–Target Interaction Prediction using Graph-Transformer Embeddings and Multi-Omics Integration
results.venue: International conference Computer Networks and Inventive Communication Technologies

results.title: PRFold-TNN: Protein Fold Recognition With an Ensemble Feature Selection Method Using PageRank Algorithm Based on Transformer
results.venue: IEEE/ACM Transactions on Computational Biology & Bioinformatics

results.title: ProtAgent-ESM2: an Efficient Framework for Long Protein Sequences Modeling in Protein-Protein Interaction Prediction
results.venue: IEEE International Conference on Bioinformatics and Biomedicine

results.title: An Effective Framework for Protein Fold Prediction through the Fusion of Evolutionary Information and Attention Mechanism with Deep Neural Networks.
results.venue: IEEE journal of biomedical and health informatics
results.tldr: This work marks a notable progression in Protein Fold Recognition by combining evolutionary features with attention mechanisms, leading to improved prediction accuracy.
results.abstract: This work marks a notable progression in Protein Fold Recognition by combining evolutionary features with attention mechanisms, leading to improved prediction accuracy.
